article.body_preview: DHAKA - Bangladesh has been experiencing unprecedented load-shedding for more than two weeks now, and the scale of the current crisis certainly demands an answer from the power sector officials. People have been facing 2,000-2,500MW of load-shedding throughout the day. It is expected to get even worse with the Payra Power Plant shutting down last week. Routine excuses for failure from the power ministry cite the global fuel crisis as well as the dollar crunch, which has made it difficult to open

llm_connections.evidence_quote: the dollar crunch, which has made it difficult to open letters of credit (LCs) to import fuel and coal.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly links LC availability (letters of credit) to the ability to import fuel and coal, matching the feature's driver-level scope on imports driven by LC availability.
